blurb: iCloud stores user data across service categories with varying encryption models. Standard data categories are encrypted at rest using keys Apple holds for service operation. End-to-end encrypted categories – including Health, iCloud Keychain, and most others under Advanced Data Protection – use keys derived exclusively from device hardware that Apple cannot access. CloudKit end-to-end encryption uses asymmetric keys generated on the user's devices, with public keys registered to Apple's Identity Service and private keys held only in device secure storage. iCloud Backup optionally backs up the Data Protection keybag, escrowed to enable account recovery.
